preview_text:
11

You hit bottom when you stop digging.

—the Big Book

Brighton Beach

Now

We make our way down the boardwalk, the shuttered attractions of Coney Island disappearing into the night sky behind us. The unencumbered wind hurtling off the water bites and claws at our skin.

We could have walked down Surf Avenue, gotten a little shelter from the weather, but I think Booker and Valencia feel the same way I do: the cold air is clarifying, and we’re all processing the weight of what could
